lines.en_lit: Although God will enlarge Japheth, the Divine Presence rests only in the tents of Shem,

From where do we the Persians descend from Japheth? As it is written: “The sons of Japheth were Gomer and Magog and Madai and Javan and Tuval and Meshech and Tiras” Gomer, that is Germamya; Magog, that is Kandiya; Madai, that is Macedonia; Javan, in accordance with its plain meaning, Tuval, that is Beit Unaiki; Meshech, that is Musya. Tiras, Rabbi Simai and the Rabbis disagree, and some say Rabbi Simon and the Rabbis: One said: That is Beit Teraiki, and one said: That is Persia. Rav Yosef taught: Tiras is Persia.
Sabtah and Raamah and Sabteca” Rav Yosef taught: the inner Sakistan and the outer Sakistan. Between one and the other one hundred parasangs, and the circumference one thousand parasangs.

“And the beginning of his kingdom was Babel, and Erech, and Accad, and Calneh, Babel in accordance with its plain meaning, Erech, that is Orikhut; and Accad, that is Baskar; Calneh, that is Nofer Ninefi.

“Out of that land went forth Asshur” Rav Yosef taught: Asshur, that is Silek, “And built Nineveh and Rehoboth-ir and Calah” Nineveh, in accordance with its plain meaning; Rehovoth-ir, that is Perat of Meishan; Calah, that is Perat of Bursif. “And Resen between Nineveh and Calah, it is the great city” Resen, that is Akteisfon. It is the great city; I do not know whether Nineveh the great city, whether Resen the great city. When it says: “And Nineveh was a great city of God, a three-day journey across” you must say Nineveh is the great city.

“And there were Ahiman, Sheshai, and Talmai, the children of Anak” It was taught: Ahiman skillful [meyuman] of his brothers. Sheshai he renders the ground like pits sheḥitot Talmai he renders the ground furrows [telamim] Alternatively: Ahiman built Anat; Sheshai built Alush; Talmai built Talbush. The children of Anak that the sun is a necklace [shema’anikin] their height.

Rabbi Yehoshua ben Levi said Rabbi said: Rome is destined to fall into the hands of Persia, as it is stated: “Now hear the plan that the Lord has devised for Edom, and the thoughts He has considered for the residents of Teiman. Surely the youngest of the flock will drag them away, surely their habitation will be appalled due to them”

Rabba bar Ulla strongly objected to this. where inferred that this Youngest of the flock, is Persia? as it is written: “The ram that you saw sporting two horns are the kings of Media and Persia” And say Greece, as it is written: “The goat is the king of Greece”

When Rav Ḥaviva bar Surmakei ascended he stated before a certain one of the Sages. said to him: One who does not know how to interpret verses raises an objection to Rabbi What The youngest of the flock? the youngest of the brothers, as Rav Yosef taught: Tiras, that is Persia.

Rabba bar bar Ḥana said Rabbi Yoḥanan said in the name of Rabbi Yehuda, son of Rabbi Elai: Rome is destined to fall into the hands of Persia. an a fortiori Just as the First Temple, that the descendants of Shem built it and the Chaldeans destroyed it, the Chaldeans, fell to Persians, the Second Temple, that the Persians built it and the Romans destroyed it, is it not right that the Romans will fall into the hands of the Persians?

Rav said: Persia is destined to fall into the hands of Rome. Rav Kahana and Rav Asi, said to Rav: The builders into the hands of the destroyers? He said to them: yes, that is the King’s decree. Some say he said to them: They, too, are destroyers of synagogues,

That was also taught Persia is destined to fall into the hands of Rome. One they destroyed synagogues. And furthermore, it is the King’s decree that the builders into the hands of the destroyers, as Rav Yehuda said Rav said: The son of David will come only when the wicked kingdom of Rome spreads throughout the world for nine months, as it is stated: “Therefore He will give them up until she who is to bear has borne; then the remnants of his brethren will return with the children of Israel”
The Rabbis taught: None of the chambers in the Temple had a mezuza except for the Chamber of Parhedrin, in which there was a place of residence of the High Priest.
Rabbi Yehuda said: weren’t there several chambers in the Temple in which there was a place of residence they did not have a mezuza? Rather, the Chamber of Parhedrin was a decree.

What is the reason of Rabbi Yehuda Rava said Rabbi Yehuda holds: any house that is not designated for the summer and for the rainy season is not a house Abaye raised an objection to his Isn’t it written: “I will strike the winter-house with the summer-house” said to him: called the winter-house or the summer-house. It is not called a house unmodified.

Abaye raised a objection to the sukka the festival on the festival Rabbi Yehuda obligates and the Rabbis exempt And it was taught concerning Rabbi Yehuda obligates in joining and in mezuza and in tithes
And if you say by rabbinic law granted, joining and mezuza, it is to say by rabbinic law; however, tithes, is it to say by rabbinic law?
